headword_jp: {近|きん}{接|せつ}{攻|こう}{撃|げき}

definitions.gloss_en: an attack performed in physical contact or at very short range with the target
definitions.gloss_jp: {目|もく}{標|ひょう}と{接|せっ}{触|しょく}または{至|し}{近|きん}{距|きょ}{離|り}で{行|おこな}う{攻|こう}{撃|げき}
definitions.explanation_en: An attack requiring the attacker to be adjacent to or in base contact with the target. Includes swords, axes, claws, and other hand-to-hand weapons.
definitions.implicit_notes: 近接 (close/adjacent) is a widely-used Japanese word across gaming, military, and everyday language. The compound is descriptive and not proprietary.
definitions.orthography_notes: 近接: 近 (near) + 接 (contact/touch). 攻撃: attack. Fire Emblem uses the 近接 vs. 遠距離 weapon distinction as a core mechanic. JWC documentation attests 近接 in wargame contexts. Not WH-specific.
definitions.game_setting_notes: In miniature wargames, 近接攻撃 is resolved during the 白兵戦段階. In video games (SRPGs), 近接武器 (melee weapon) is a standard category.

usage_jp: {近|きん}{接|せつ}{攻|こう}{撃|げき}を{宣|せん}{言|げん}する (declare a melee attack); {近|きん}{接|せつ}{武|ぶ}{器|き} (melee weapon)
comparative_notes: 遠距離攻撃 is the antonym. In Fire Emblem, 近接 and 遠距離 are the two primary weapon-range categories. In miniature wargames, 近接攻撃 occurs in the 白兵戦段階.

examples.japanese: {白|はく}{兵|へい}{戦|せん}{段|だん}{階|かい}に{近|きん}{接|せつ}{攻|こう}{撃|げき}を{実|じっ}{行|こう}する。
examples.english: Execute a melee attack during the fighting phase.

cross_references.note: 白兵戦段階 — the phase in which melee attacks occur
